fields.description: Huanglongbing is one of the most destructive citrus diseases caused by bacteria spread by psyllid insects.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Yellow mottled leaves
Uneven fruit coloration
Small fruits
Premature fruit drop
fields.prevention:
Control psyllid insects
Remove infected trees
Use certified planting material
Regular monitoring
fields.recommendation: Monitor orchards regularly for symptoms, control Asian citrus psyllid populations using integrated pest management, remove severely infected trees, and always use certified disease-free planting material.

fields.description: Late Blight is a destructive disease caused by Phytophthora infestans and can rapidly destroy tomato crops.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Water-soaked lesions
Brown leaf spots
White fungal growth
Fruit rot
fields.prevention:
Use resistant varieties
Avoid excess moisture
Apply fungicides
fields.recommendation: Immediately remove infected plants, avoid overhead irrigation, improve field drainage, and apply protective fungicides during cool and wet conditions.

fields.description: Apple Scab is a fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis that affects leaves and fruits, reducing fruit quality and market value.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Olive-green leaf spots
Dark scabby lesions on fruits
Premature leaf drop
Reduced fruit quality
fields.prevention:
Use resistant varieties
Prune trees for airflow
Remove fallen leaves
Apply fungicides when necessary
fields.recommendation: Collect fallen leaves, improve canopy ventilation, avoid prolonged leaf wetness, and apply fungicides during early spring if recommended.

fields.description: Black Rot is a fungal disease that infects leaves, branches, and fruits causing severe crop losses.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Purple leaf spots
Fruit rot
Cankers on branches
Shriveled fruits
fields.prevention:
Remove infected plant material
Maintain orchard sanitation
Prune affected branches
Use fungicides
fields.recommendation: Remove infected fruits and branches, sanitize orchard debris, improve airflow, and apply fungicides when necessary.

fields.description: A fungal disease requiring both apple and cedar hosts to complete its life cycle.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Bright orange leaf spots
Premature leaf drop
Fruit blemishes
Reduced vigor
fields.prevention:
Remove nearby cedar hosts
Use resistant cultivars
Apply preventive fungicides
fields.recommendation: Remove nearby juniper hosts where possible, prune infected branches, and apply preventive fungicides during susceptible growth stages.

fields.description: The apple plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
fields.signs_of_damage: No significant disease symptoms detected
fields.prevention:
Continue good irrigation and nutrient management
Monitor regularly for diseases
fields.recommendation: The apple tree appears healthy. Maintain proper pruning, balanced nutrition, and routine orchard monitoring for sustained productivity.

fields.description: The blueberry plant appears healthy and disease-free.
fields.signs_of_damage: No visible disease symptoms
fields.prevention:
Maintain proper watering and soil pH
Monitor regularly
fields.recommendation: The blueberry plant appears healthy. Continue regular irrigation, mulching, and periodic monitoring for pests and diseases.

fields.description: Powdery Mildew is a fungal disease that affects leaves, shoots, and fruits.
fields.signs_of_damage:
White powdery growth
Leaf curling
Reduced photosynthesis
Fruit quality reduction
fields.prevention:
Improve air circulation
Avoid excessive nitrogen
Apply fungicides
fields.recommendation: Improve airflow through pruning, avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization, remove infected shoots, and apply fungicides if required.

fields.description: The cherry plant appears healthy and free from disease.
fields.signs_of_damage: No disease symptoms observed
fields.prevention: Continue standard orchard management practices
fields.recommendation: The cherry tree appears healthy. Continue seasonal pruning, balanced irrigation, and orchard sanitation to maintain fruit quality.

fields.description: A fungal disease causing rectangular gray lesions that reduce photosynthesis.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Gray rectangular lesions
Leaf blighting
Reduced vigor
fields.prevention:
Crop rotation
Resistant hybrids
Fungicide applications
fields.recommendation: Practice crop rotation, reduce crop residue, improve field drainage, and use recommended fungicides if disease severity increases.

fields.description: Common Rust is caused by Puccinia sorghi and produces reddish-brown pustules.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Rust-colored pustules
Leaf weakening
Reduced grain filling
fields.prevention:
Use resistant hybrids
Monitor fields
Apply fungicides if needed
fields.recommendation: Monitor disease progression regularly, plant resistant hybrids, and apply fungicides when infection reaches economic thresholds.

fields.description: A major fungal disease causing long cigar-shaped lesions on leaves.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Long gray-green lesions
Leaf death
Reduced photosynthesis
fields.prevention:
Crop rotation
Resistant hybrids
Field sanitation
fields.recommendation: Use resistant hybrids, rotate crops, remove infected crop residues, and apply fungicides when disease pressure becomes severe.

fields.description: The corn crop appears healthy with no disease symptoms.
fields.signs_of_damage: No visible disease symptoms
fields.prevention: Continue balanced nutrient and irrigation management
fields.recommendation: The corn crop appears healthy. Continue balanced fertilization, irrigation management, and routine crop inspection throughout the growing season.

fields.description: Black Rot is a serious fungal disease affecting grape leaves, shoots, and fruits, leading to significant yield losses.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Brown circular leaf spots
Black fruit lesions
Shriveled grapes
Premature fruit drop
fields.prevention:
Remove infected plant debris
Improve air circulation
Apply fungicides
Prune regularly
fields.recommendation: Remove infected fruits and leaves, improve canopy ventilation, and apply preventive fungicides during periods of high humidity.

fields.description: Esca is a complex fungal disease that damages grapevine wood and reduces vineyard productivity.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Tiger-stripe leaf patterns
Wood discoloration
Sudden vine collapse
Poor fruit quality
fields.prevention:
Prune properly
Remove infected wood
Disinfect pruning tools
Monitor vineyard health
fields.recommendation: Prune infected wood during dry weather, disinfect pruning tools, remove severely infected vines, and maintain vineyard sanitation.

fields.description: Leaf Blight causes necrotic lesions on grape leaves and reduces photosynthetic activity.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Brown leaf spots
Leaf drying
Premature defoliation
Reduced vigor
fields.prevention:
Use disease-free planting material
Maintain airflow
Apply fungicides
fields.recommendation: Remove infected leaves, improve airflow within the vineyard, avoid prolonged leaf wetness, and apply fungicides if required.

fields.description: The grapevine appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
fields.signs_of_damage: No disease symptoms detected
fields.prevention: Continue vineyard monitoring and proper irrigation practices
fields.recommendation: The grapevine appears healthy. Continue proper pruning, irrigation, and canopy management while monitoring for disease symptoms.

Dark leaf spots
Fruit lesions
Leaf drop
Reduced fruit quality
fields.prevention:
Use resistant cultivars
Avoid overhead irrigation
Apply copper-based sprays
fields.recommendation: Prune infected branches, improve air circulation, avoid overhead watering, and apply copper-based bactericides during early infection stages.

fields.description: The peach plant appears healthy and disease-free.

fields.prevention: Maintain regular orchard care and monitoring
fields.recommendation: The peach tree appears healthy. Continue seasonal pruning, balanced fertilization, and regular orchard inspections to maintain productivity.

Copper sprays

White powdery patches
Leaf yellowing

Avoid overcrowding
Apply fungicides
Remove infected leaves

Brown leaf margins
Leaf drying
Reduced fruit production
fields.prevention:
Use disease-free planting material
Remove infected leaves
Improve drainage
fields.recommendation: Remove infected leaves, improve field sanitation, avoid excessive moisture, and apply appropriate fungicides if disease progresses.

fields.description: The strawberry plant appears healthy and disease-free.

fields.recommendation: The strawberry plant appears healthy. Continue proper irrigation, mulching, and regular field inspection to maintain optimal plant growth.

fields.description: Bacterial Spot is caused by Xanthomonas bacteria and affects leaves, stems, and fruits.

Dark leaf spots
Yellow halos

Use certified seeds
Crop rotation
Copper sprays
Avoid overhead irrigation

fields.description: Early Blight is a fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani that affects tomato foliage and fruits.

Concentric ring lesions
Yellowing leaves
Leaf drop
Reduced fruit production
fields.prevention:
Crop rotation
Remove infected debris
Apply fungicides

fields.description: Leaf Mold is a fungal disease common in humid greenhouse conditions.

Yellow leaf patches
Olive-green mold
Leaf curling
Reduced photosynthesis
fields.prevention:
Improve ventilation
Reduce humidity
Use resistant varieties
fields.recommendation: Reduce humidity by improving ventilation, avoid excessive watering, and apply recommended fungicides if disease severity increases.

Small circular spots
Leaf yellowing
Premature leaf drop

Crop rotation
Remove infected leaves
Apply fungicides
fields.recommendation: Remove infected leaves, avoid wetting foliage during irrigation, rotate crops annually, and apply fungicides early when symptoms appear.

fields.description: Spider Mites are tiny pests that damage tomato plants by feeding on leaf tissues.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Yellow stippling
Leaf bronzing
Webbing
Leaf drop
fields.prevention:
Use biological controls
Maintain humidity
Apply miticides when required
fields.recommendation: Monitor plants frequently for mite infestations, maintain adequate humidity, introduce beneficial predatory mites, or apply approved miticides when necessary.

Brown circular lesions
Leaf blight
Fruit spotting
fields.prevention:
Improve airflow
Crop rotation
Apply fungicides
fields.recommendation: Improve air circulation by proper plant spacing, avoid overhead irrigation, and apply recommended fungicides during humid conditions to reduce disease spread.

fields.description: A viral disease transmitted by whiteflies that severely affects tomato growth and yield.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Leaf curling
Yellowing leaves
Stunted growth
Poor fruit set
fields.prevention:
Control whiteflies
Use resistant varieties
Remove infected plants
fields.recommendation: Control whitefly populations using integrated pest management. Remove infected plants promptly and use resistant tomato varieties whenever possible.

fields.description: Tomato Mosaic Virus is a viral disease causing mottled leaves and reduced crop productivity.
fields.signs_of_damage:
Mosaic leaf patterns
Leaf distortion
Reduced vigor
Poor fruit quality
fields.prevention:
Use disease-free seeds
Disinfect tools
Remove infected plants
fields.recommendation: Remove infected plants immediately to prevent virus spread. Disinfect gardening tools, control aphids, and use certified virus-free seeds for future cultivation.

fields.description: The tomato plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
